response.justification: Bogdan is a seasoned full-stack and TypeScript/Node.js engineer with genuine geospatial exposure through GISCollective and Green Map, and some low-latency systems work at Dunnhumby. However, the role demands deep, production-grade C++ or Rust as its core requirement, and Bogdan's only C++ experience is a brief six-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 (plus a bachelor thesis project) — C++ does not even appear in his listed skills. He has no routing tile pipeline experience, no navigation guidance system background, and no Rust exposure whatsoever. The geospatial work on his resume centers on GIS data collection and mapping UIs, which is architecturally far removed from high-throughput routing tile generation and real-time traffic integration.
strengths: Real geospatial domain exposure through GISCollective and Green Map GIS platform, including FOSS4G conference presentation
Demonstrated experience building low-latency, high-throughput distributed services (Dunnhumby: millions of HTTP req/s with async I/O and caching)
Broad systems breadth — Linux, Docker, Kubernetes, event-driven architectures, and comfort picking new languages per problem domain
concerns: No meaningful modern C++ or Rust experience; primary language stack is TypeScript/Node.js/JavaScript with only superficial, decade-old C++ exposure
Geospatial experience is GIS data collection and mapping UI, not routing tile generation, map data transformation, or navigation pipeline engineering
No background in navigation guidance systems, routing accuracy, or the QA rigor specific to production routing data at global scale
